headlines.title: New footage shows how Trump dinner gunman charged through security in four seconds
headlines.description: The CCTV shows an officer draw a firearm and open fire as the suspect sprints past.

headlines.title: Billions of meals at risk due to Iran war, says fertiliser boss
headlines.description: A shortage of fertiliser due to the Iran conflict could reduce crop yields and push prices higher, says the boss of Yara.

headlines.title: Labour's London squeeze exposes a fragmented British politics
headlines.description: London offers an insight into the dilemma Labour faces about which direction to take.

headlines.title: The Renters' Rights Act is here - this is what it means for tenants and landlords
headlines.description: The biggest shake up of renting rules in England for 30 years affects millions of people.

headlines.title: Why hundreds of everyday medicines are now so hard to get
headlines.description: People living with conditions include heart problems, stroke risks, eye infections and bipolar are unable to get hold of the drugs they rely on.

headlines.title: Chippies sell catfish as 'traditional fish supper'
headlines.description: A BBC investigation finds chip shop owners passing off cheaper species as "traditional fish and chips".

headlines.title: British couple jailed in Iran: 'We're likely to be here for a long time'
headlines.description: Lindsay and Craig Foreman are facing the reality of a 10-year prison sentence following their arrest in Iran while on a motorcycle tour last year.

headlines.title: Met chief defends knife attack officers after criticism from Zack Polanski
headlines.description: Sir Mark Rowley says he is "disappointed" that Green Party leader Zack Polanski shared a post condemning how police subdued the suspect.

headlines.title: King arrives in Bermuda after ending US trip with visit to small town America
headlines.description: On the final day of the state visit, the royal couple headed to Virginia for a more informal experience of the US.

headlines.title: Oscar goes missing after Academy Award winner is blocked from taking it on flight
headlines.description: The airline said it regrets the situation and was doing an urgent "comprehensive internal search" for the award.

headlines.title: How MI5 foiled a Tesco worker's plan for mass gun attack
headlines.description: Police say Alfie Coleman appeared to be a normal teenager but was hiding a dark plan.

headlines.title: I get to work on the most scenic railway line in the world
headlines.description: A proposal to close the line was rejected after thousands of people - and one dog - objected.

headlines.title: Newspaper headlines: 'PM vows to act' and 'Brace for more terror attacks'
headlines.description: The main story for many of the papers continues to be the knife attack in Golders Green on Wednesday.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Who walked off the I'm A Celebrity live final?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: Is Saudi Arabia's sports revolution unravelling?
headlines.description: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf, but what does this mean for the country's other sporting investments? Dan Roan takes a closer look.

headlines.title: Pickleball and protests: How a Trump visit is roiling the world's largest retirement community
headlines.description: Politics have become an increasingly thorny issue in the Villages, the world's largest retirement community.

headlines.title: 'There were letters I didn't want to open': Rise in unpaid debt court cases
headlines.description: The number of county court judgements rose by 17.5% in the first quarter of this year compared to last, data suggests.

headlines.title: May's full 'Flower Moon' will light up the sky tonight
headlines.description: With spring flowers in full bloom, May's full Flower Moon rises on Friday as Darren Bett explains.

headlines.title: One of the world's most valuable books goes on display in Glasgow
headlines.description: Birds of America by John James Audubon goes on the display in the Royal College of Physicians and Surgeons of Glasgow.

headlines.title: 'So stressful' - teachers and pupils say new GCSEs are leading to burnout
headlines.description: Unions warn teachers will quit and there are fears for pupils' wellbeing as classroom tests increase.

headlines.title: Britney Spears charged with driving under influence in California
headlines.description: The pop star is facing one misdemeanour count of driving under the influence of any alcohol and drug.

headlines.title: South East Water chair resigns after report into supply issues that left thousands without drinking water
headlines.description: It comes after a group of MPs declared that they had no confidence in the company's leadership.

headlines.title: Has the royal state visit saved the special relationship?
headlines.description: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at whether King Charles' charm offensive with President Trump made a difference to US-UK relations.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Week: The Price Of The Iran War
headlines.description: Could financial uncertainty from the Iran war stop a Starmer leadership challenge?

headlines.title: LIVE at Maida Vale studios… How popular is Donald Trump in the US?
headlines.description: And why isn’t there more public support for Democrats?

headlines.title: Is Saudi Arabia's sports revolution unravelling?
headlines.description: Saudi Arabia will withdraw its multi-billion dollar backing of LIV Golf, but what does this mean for the country's other sporting investments? Dan Roan takes a closer look.

headlines.title: Why was Mudryk given ban in secret and what happens now?
headlines.description: Chelsea winger Mykhailo Mudryk is appealing against a four-year doping ban but why has the process been kept secret, and what happens now?

headlines.title: 'Where are you? Wow. It is so, so bad' - Emery's impassioned VAR rant
headlines.description: Aston Villa boss Unai Emery says VAR made a "huge mistake" in not sending off Nottingham Forest's Elliot Anderson in their Europa League semi-final first leg.

headlines.title: 'If Kane scored that we'd be saying wow' - Strand Larsen shows Palace worth
headlines.description: Being a club's record signing brings with it pressure, but Jorgen Strand Larsen puts a difficult start behind him with a goal that could be crucial in Crystal Palace's pursuit of a first major European trophy.

headlines.title: Littler beats Humphries to go top of Premier League
headlines.description: World number one Luke Littler wins his fifth night of the Premier League Darts season to return to the top of the table.
